name: apksigner
title: apksigner
summary: command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs
description:
command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs
apksig is a project which aims to simplify APK signing and checking
whether APK's signatures should verify on Android. apksig supports
JAR signing (used by Android since day one) and APK Signature Scheme
v2 (supported since Android Nougat, API Level 24).
.
The key feature of apksig is that it knows about differences in APK
signature verification logic between different versions of the
Android platform. apksig can thus check whether a signed APK is
expected to verify on all Android platform versions supported by the
APK. When signing an APK, apksig will choose the most appropriate
cryptographic algorithms based on the Android platform versions
supported by the APK being signed.
.
apksigner command-line tool offers two operations:
.
* sign the provided APK so that it verifies on all Android platforms
supported by the APK. Run apksigner sign for usage information.
.
* check whether the provided APK's signatures are expected to verify
on all Android platforms supported by the APK. Run apksigner verify
for usage information.
.
The tool determines the range of Android platform versions (API
Levels) supported by the APK by inspecting the APK's
AndroidManifest.xml. This behavior can be overridden by specifying
the range of platform versions on the command-line.

command_examples: apksigner  sign  --ks release.jks app.apk apksigner verify --verbose app.apk apksigner lineage
Level 15 and higher: $ apksigner verify --min-sdk-version 15 app.apk
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use apksigner because command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use apksigner as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apksigner  [options] apk` as documented upstream for apksigner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apksigner --version` as documented upstream for apksigner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apksigner --help` as documented upstream for apksigner.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `apksigner  sign  --ks release.jks app.apk apksigner verify --verbose app.apk apksigner lineage` as documented upstream for apksigner.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair apksigner with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `apksigner --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: apksigner - sign and verify Android APKs
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install android-platform-tools-apksig. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `apksigner --version`. Further invocations from upstream: `apksigner --help`; `apksigner  sign  --ks release.jks app.apk apksigner verify --verbose app.apk apksigner lineage`. command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: command line tool to sign and verify Android APKs.
